description: Add missing unit tests for high-priority untested source files and expand E2E test coverage for critical gaps. Focuses on files with complex logic that currently have zero test coverage, and E2E paths that represent production risks.

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for untested browser tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for the 5 most important untested browser tools so that their full-stack behavior is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_clear_console_logs: test that clearing console logs after capturing some returns empty list
browser_get_resource_content: test that fetching a known page resource (e.g., a CSS file) returns content
extension_get_state: test that it returns state object with connection status, plugin count, and tab count
extension_get_logs: test that it returns recent extension log entries
extension_get_side_panel: test that it returns side panel state (open/closed, connected)
All new tests are added to e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts following the existing test.describe pattern
Each test follows existing fixture pattern: async ({ mcpServer, testServer, extensionContext, mcpClient })
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes (at minimum the new tests pass)
userStories.notes: File: e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts. Follow the exact pattern of existing browser tool tests in this file. Each test: (1) opens a tab to the test server, (2) calls the tool via mcpClient.callTool(), (3) asserts the result. For extension_get_state/get_logs/get_side_panel, no test server tab is needed — just call the tool directly. For browser_clear_console_logs: first call browser_get_console_logs to verify entries exist, then clear, then verify empty. For browser_get_resource_content: navigate to a page that loads a CSS or JS resource, then request it.

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for plugin discovery edge cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for plugin discovery error scenarios so that broken plugins don't crash the server.
acceptanceCriteria: Test that a local plugin path pointing to non-existent directory appears in /health failedPlugins with clear error
Test that a local plugin with missing dist/tools.json appears in failedPlugins
Test that a local plugin with invalid JSON in dist/tools.json appears in failedPlugins
Test that a local plugin with missing dist/adapter.iife.js appears in failedPlugins
Test that valid plugins still load correctly when one plugin in localPlugins is broken
Test that after fixing a broken plugin and calling POST /reload, it transitions from failedPlugins to healthy
All new tests follow existing fixture patterns from e2e/health-endpoint.e2e.ts
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes (at minimum the new tests pass)
userStories.notes: Add tests to a new file: e2e/discovery-edge-cases.e2e.ts. Follow the pattern from e2e/health-endpoint.e2e.ts which already tests failedPlugins for non-existent paths. The key is creating temp directories with intentionally broken plugin structures. Use the standalone fixture approach from e2e/config-watcher.e2e.ts which manages its own MCP server and config. For 'fixing a broken plugin': write a valid tools.json after the initial broken state, then POST /reload and verify /health.

userStories.notes: File: e2e/resources-prompts.e2e.ts. Some of these tests already exist partially — check the file first to avoid duplicates. The existing tests cover 'read resource with nonexistent URI' and 'get prompt with nonexistent name'. Focus on the NEW gaps: missing required arguments for typed prompts (the typed_greet prompt requires checking), and empty lists when no plugins define resources/prompts (would need a fixture without the e2e-test plugin's resources/prompts, or a separate test that unloads the plugin first).
